briefing.headline: Crank it — fall clear water calls for steady cranking on key structure.
briefing.gameplan:
In fall with clear water and stable conditions, cranking presentations shine.

KVD: 'Red Eye Shad is at its best in cold water. It always makes bottom contact and finds inside weed edges. Ripping it free triggers strikes. It has a shimmy as it sinks like a stick worm — dynamite for yo-yoing inside edges. Sweep rod to the side, let it sink, repeat, moving bait 4-6 feet each swe

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'Red Eye Shad is at its best in cold water. It always makes bottom contact and finds inside weed edges. Ripping it free triggers strikes. It has a shimmy as it sinks like a stick worm — dynamite
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: Even in slick calm clear conditions, a jerkbait over the top of them will draw fish from distance. 'They're going to see it and they're going to come.' Don't switch to finesse just because it's c
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Biggest smallmouth cranking difference: it's NOT about bottom contact. Keep crankbait a few feet off bottom — smallmouth in clear water love to feed looking up. It's about speed and covering water, casting all directions to find roaming aggr...
Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable fall conditions create the most predictable fall fishing. Bass follow shad migrations into creek arms and pockets, feeding at 3-15ft. Key on points at creek mouths and secondary flats where bait concentrates.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize lures and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
